q.en: Who is Jude McNally, interviewed in the book?
q.zh: 书中接受采访的 Jude McNally 是谁？

options.en: Only a spider expert according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 只是一位蜘蛛专家（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Managing director of the Arizona Poison and Drug Information Center
options.zh: 亚利桑那毒物与药物信息中心主任

explain.en: The Introduction identifies him as 'the managing director of the Arizona Poison and Drug Information Center in Tucson, Arizona.'
explain.zh: 引言介绍他是“亚利桑那州图森市毒物与药物信息中心的主任”。

q.en: The book says doctors look for symptoms from four life-threatening culprits. Which is one of them?
q.zh: 书中说医生会排查四种致命“元凶”的症状。以下哪一个是其中之一？

options.en: Houseflies according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 家蝇（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Jude lists the four culprits as 'black widow spiders, scorpions, insects that fly and sting, and rattlesnakes.'
explain.zh: Jude 列出的四种元凶是“黑寡妇蜘蛛、蝎子、会飞且蜇人的昆虫，以及响尾蛇”。

options.en: A kind of antivenom according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一种抗毒血清（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Poisonous liquid from an animal such as a snake, spider, or insect
options.zh: 来自动物（如蛇、蜘蛛或昆虫）的有毒液体

explain.en: The glossary defines 'venom (n.)' as 'poisonous liquid from an animal, such as a snake, spider, or insect (p. 6).'
explain.zh: 词汇表把 venom 定义为“来自动物（如蛇、蜘蛛或昆虫）的有毒液体（第6页）”。

q.en: A key sign of a black widow spider bite is...
q.zh: 黑寡妇蜘蛛咬伤的一个关键迹象是……

options.en: Muscle cramping, especially in small children
options.zh: 肌肉痉挛，尤其在小孩身上明显

options.en: A fever only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只是发烧（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says black widow bites cause 'Muscle cramping' and 'We rule out black widow spider bites when there isn't any muscle cramping.'
explain.zh: 书中说黑寡妇咬伤会引起“肌肉痉挛”，并且“如果没有肌肉痉挛，就排除黑寡妇咬伤”。

q.en: In Arizona, which scorpion can be life-threatening?
q.zh: 在亚利桑那州，哪种蝎子可能致命？

explain.en: The book says: 'We have over thirty different kinds of scorpions in Arizona, and only the bark scorpion can be life threatening.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“亚利桑那有三十多种蝎子，只有树皮蝎可能致命。”

q.en: With bees, wasps, and ants, the most dangerous problem is...
q.zh: 被蜜蜂、黄蜂和蚂蚁蜇伤时，最危险的问题是……

options.en: A single puncture wound according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一个穿刺伤口（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A type 1 allergic reaction affecting the whole body
options.zh: 影响全身的1型过敏反应

explain.en: The book warns a reaction 'anywhere other than at the site within the first hour' is 'a type 1 allergic reaction that can be life threatening.'
explain.zh: 书中警告，若“第一小时内在伤口以外任何地方出现反应”，就是“可能致命的1型过敏反应”。

q.en: According to Jude, more people die from ___ than from black widows, scorpions, and rattlesnakes combined.
q.zh: 根据 Jude 的说法，死于___的人比死于黑寡妇、蝎子和响尾蛇的总和还多。

options.en: Spider bites according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 蜘蛛咬伤（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states: 'More people die from insect stings than from black widows, scorpions, and rattlesnakes combined.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“死于昆虫蜇伤的人，比死于黑寡妇、蝎子和响尾蛇的总和还多。”

q.en: A safety tip Jude gives is to...
q.zh: Jude 给出的安全建议是……

options.en: Touch unknown animals according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 触摸不认识的动物（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Turn on lights at night and step back from danger
options.zh: 夜间开灯，并远离危险退后一步

explain.en: The Conclusion advises: 'Turning on a light at night is a good safety measure' and 'take one step back' to leave danger.
explain.zh: 结语建议：“夜间开灯是一个好的安全措施”，并“退后一步”以远离危险。

q.en: The author included this interview mainly to...
q.zh: 作者收录这篇采访的主要目的是……

options.en: Inform readers how to recognize dangerous bites/stings and stay safe
options.zh: 告诉读者如何识别危险的咬伤/蜇伤并保持安全

options.en: Entertain with a fictional tale according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 用虚构故事娱乐读者（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book is an interview with a poison expert giving clear, practical safety and identification advice.
explain.zh: 这本书是对毒物专家的采访，提供清晰、实用的安全与识别建议。

q.en: Which of these animals can carry rabies?
q.zh: 以下哪些动物可能携带狂犬病？

options.en: Skunks, bats, raccoons, foxes, and coyotes
options.zh: 臭鼬、蝙蝠、浣熊、狐狸和土狼

options.en: Lizards and frogs according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 蜥蜴和青蛙（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The Rabies section says 'skunks, bats, raccoons, foxes, and coyotes all can carry rabies,' while cold-blooded animals cannot.
explain.zh: “狂犬病”一节说“臭鼬、蝙蝠、浣熊、狐狸和土狼都可能携带狂犬病”，而冷血动物不会。

prompt.en: Make a simple 'Desert Safety' poster listing two things to DO and two things NOT to do around bites and stings, based on Jude's advice.
prompt.zh: 根据 Jude 的建议，制作一张简单的“沙漠安全”海报，列出两条“该做”和两条“不该做”的防咬伤/蜇伤事项。

en: DO: turn on lights at night, step back and leave animals alone.
DON'T: touch or harass unknown animals; ignore a whole-body allergic reaction.
zh: 该做：夜间开灯、退后并远离动物。
不该做：触摸或骚扰不认识的动物；忽视全身性过敏反应。
